parallel--0a716604-63d5-4398-9a4d-70bfbf.name: Configure detection rules to identify anomalous ICMP or UDP activity

description: This is an automatically generated playbook based on the question: A detections playbook for T1095 of MITRE ATT&CK Matrix for Enterprise framework is required.The target platform is Windows. You can find more information at https://attack.mitre.org/detectionstrategies/DET0457#AN1254 . The description of the approach follows: Anomalous use of ICMP or UDP by non-network service processes for data exfiltration or remote control, especially if traffic bypasses proxy infrastructure or shows unusual flow patterns.

external_references.name: MITRE ATT&CK T1095: Non-Application Layer Protocol

items.content: Deploy and configure network monitoring tools to capture and analyze ICMP and UDP traffic across the enterprise network.
Establish a baseline of normal ICMP and UDP traffic patterns, including typical source/destination IPs, ports, packet sizes, and frequencies for known network services and processes.
Implement process-to-network correlation by monitoring process IDs associated with network connections using EDR, Sysmon, or similar endpoint monitoring solutions.
Detect ICMP/UDP traffic from processes not typically associated with network services (e.g., word.exe, excel.exe, user-initiated processes)
Identify traffic that bypasses proxy servers or corporate gateways (direct internet connections)
Flag unusual flow patterns: high frequency of small packets, large data transfers via ICMP/UDP, traffic to rare external IPs or domains
Detect ICMP tunneling patterns: consistent payload sizes, encrypted payloads, or protocol misuse (e.g., ICMP echo requests with data)
Configure detection rules to identify anomalous ICMP or UDP activity
Correlate network events with endpoint process creation events to identify the originating process and user context.
Implement alerting for detected anomalies with appropriate severity levels based on confidence and impact.
Conduct initial triage of alerts: verify process legitimacy, check digital signatures, and review user/host history.
If malicious activity is suspected, isolate the affected endpoint from the network to prevent further data exfiltration.
Collect and preserve evidence: network packet captures, endpoint process memory, and relevant logs for forensic analysis.
Escalate to incident response team for full investigation and remediation if confirmed malicious.
